subject: Product Management
title: How Virality Works
description: A fast breakdown of How Virality Works for builders.

hook.text: Hotmail grew from zero to 12 million users in 18 months by adding one line to every email: 'Get your free email at Hotmail.'

buildup.text: Virality occurs when existing users bring in new users through normal product usage — not through advertising or marketing.

discovery.text: The viral coefficient (K-factor) measures this: if each user invites 2 people and 50% sign up, K=1.0.

twist.text: True virality is rare. Most 'viral' products actually have K < 1.0 and supplement with paid acquisition.

climax.text: Even K=0.5 is powerful: every 2 users bring 1 new user, effectively halving your customer acquisition cost.

punchline.text: Virality isn't magic. It's math: K-factor above 1.0 means unstoppable growth.

quiz.question: What does a viral coefficient (K-factor) above 1.0 mean?
options: Each user brings in more than one new user — creating exponential growth
The product has been featured in viral media
